strategy: SAME DOMAIN, SAME SLUGS. The domain (sunshineremoval.com) is NOT changing, and we preserve every legacy path verbatim with trailingSlash:true. That means for the vast majority of pages NO redirect is needed — the URL stays identical, so link equity and rankings carry over cleanly. This map exists (a) to guarantee 1:1 path parity, and (b) to catch any path that must change. Only add a 301 when a path genuinely changes.

explicit301Redirects.note: Operator-approved consolidation (2026-07-23): retired the legacy CityServicePage in favor of the data-driven /junk-removal/[city]/ system, first-wave build. Superseded its former mustPreserveExactly entry. Trailing slashes on both sides are required here to avoid a double-hop redirect chain under trailingSlash:true.

preLaunchChecklist: Run a full crawl of the LIVE site (Screaming Frog or `wget --spider`) and export every indexed URL, including any /wp-content/uploads/ image URLs and any blog/tag/category URLs not captured here. Add any missing path to mustPreserveExactly or map a 301.
Pull the top landing pages from Google Search Console (last 12 months) and confirm each still resolves 200 on the new build.
Keep the same XML sitemap path (/sitemap.xml) and resubmit in GSC after launch.
Preserve the google-site-verification meta tag (see site.json > tracking) so GSC ownership is retained.
301 any old WordPress system paths that had inbound links (e.g. /?p=ID, feed URLs) to their new equivalents or the homepage.
After DNS cutover, verify no redirect chains/loops and that http→https and non-www→(chosen host) are single-hop 301s.
